sections.content: Let me check how many blue targets remain and verify the white dot position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

# Find white dot
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"White dot at: {n['boundary']}")

# Check remaining targets
blue_1px = [(n['boundary'][0][0], n['boundary'][0][1]) for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 1]
orange_1px = [(n['boundary'][0][0], n['boundary'][0][1]) for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'O' and n['pixels'] == 1]
red_1px = [(n['boundary'][0][0], n['boundary'][0][1]) for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'r' and n['pixels'] == 1]

print(f"Blue targets remaining: {len(blue_1px)} at {blue_1px}")
print(f"Orange targets remaining: {len(orange_1px)} at {orange_1px}")
print(f"Red targets remaining: {len(red_1px)} at {red_1px}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

That's very strange. Let me reconsider what's happening. Maybe the targets are not collected by the center passing over them. Maybe something else is going on.

Actually, looking at the blue objects from earlier:

After collecting [48, 33], there were 3: [36, 27], [48, 15], [60, 27].

So [36, 27] was collected (white dot is at [36, 27] now), and [48, 33] reappeared. This is puzzling.
Hmm, wait. Maybe I'm wrong about [48, 33] being collected. Let me re-examine. When I first checked after moving to [48, 33], the blue 1px positions were [(36, 27), (48, 15), (60, 27)] - so [48, 33] was indeed collected at that point.
But now [48, 33] is back. This could mean:
